steps.title: Identify the pivot to institutional explanation
steps.content:
The passage shifts from critics' gender-focused interpretation to a different framework: the art establishment's restriction of recognition to muralists, which left nonmuralist Chicano artists—including all of Asco's members—unable to exhibit their work.

The blank should complete an interpretation that, given this institutional context, accounts for both members' presence in the piece.

steps.title: Apply the institutional context to the answer
steps.content:
Choice C states that the work reflects on constraining aesthetic expectations on Chicano artists, not women.

The other choices either restore gender as the main focus (B), falsely assign muralist grievance to a nonmuralist (A), or misidentify the critique (D).

By uniting both members through the same aesthetic constraint, C aligns with the passage's pivot to institutional context.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
